behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at the live embedding-model-to-index contract boundary, and a stubbed embedding can hide model/version misconfiguration that changes dimensionality (MOCK-HIDE). The behavior explicitly depends on a real AI/ML API output shape (LLM-DEP rule applies to model calls even with deterministic assertions). Rejected Workflow because the behavior text targets a single embedding output contract, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real configured embedding model with a fixed query, fetch the active index dimension from the vector DB metadata, and assert returned vector length equals index dimension.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily module handoff/algorithm wiring between search service and vector-repo output ordering (MOCK-MISS at module boundary), and can be caught with in-process components and deterministic fixtures. Integration-brake checks: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no dependence on AI/ML judgment quality, and no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test index/repository with known vectors, execute similarity search through service+repo wiring, and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing in cosine similarity.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the model’s ranking judgment for nuanced intent; deterministic stubs always pass and cannot verify steering effect (deterministic-assertion trap, LLM-DEP). Rejected Workflow because behavior text isolates one agentic decision point (the re-ranker) rather than validating a multi-agentic pipeline outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a fixed candidate list and nuanced query, run the real LLM re-ranker, and assert the returned order differs from raw similarity order for at least one curated case (optionally over multiple runs to account for variance).

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is relevance-quality sensitive: whether a query is ‘no close match’ depends on real embedding/model behavior plus cutoff logic. Mock embeddings can hide real false-positive neighbors (MOCK-HIDE, LLM-DEP). Rejected Workflow because the behavior text does not require validating multiple sequential agentic decisions; it can be tested at retrieval/threshold stage.
behaviors.test_description: Run out-of-domain queries through the real embedding-backed retrieval path with production relevance thresholding and assert the API returns an empty set for the curated no-match set.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: The SLO is for the full pipeline with sequential agentic stages (embedding then LLM re-rank) plus retrieval; latency failure emerges from chained steps under real runtime conditions (MULTI-STEP + REAL-INFRA + LLM-DEP). Lower-level tests miss cross-stage timing accumulation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full REST path with real embedding and re-ranking calls over a representative request sample, measure end-to-end response times, and assert median latency is <2.0s.
